data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Amazon uses a microservices architecture that breaks down its platform into small, independent services, allowing for flexibility and scalability.
data.technical_spec: Decomposed into 100s of microservices written in Java, Python, etc.

data.title: Cloud Infrastructure
data.description: The platform is hosted on Amazon Web Services (AWS), providing extensive scalability and reliability.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes AWS EC2, S3, Lambda for tiered service delivery.

data.title: Database Systems
data.description: Highly-efficient database systems are used to handle immense data volumes.
data.technical_spec: Features DynamoDB, RDS, and Redshift for various data-storage needs.

data.title: Content Delivery Network
data.description: Amazon employs a robust CDN to quickly deliver content to users around the globe.
data.technical_spec: Amazon CloudFront is integrated to serve content with low latency.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: To ensure efficient request distribution, load balancing techniques are implemented.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes Elastic Load Balancing on AWS for auto-scaling capabilities.
